description_long: This object consists of a cluster of high-rise apartment buildings arranged in a grid-like formation on a rectangular base, resembling a modern urban residential complex. The buildings are rectangular, with flat roofs and numerous evenly spaced windows on each facade, suggesting multiple residential units per floor. The structures vary in height, with some towers rising significantly taller than others, creating a dynamic skyline within the complex. The exterior surfaces are smooth and predominantly light-colored, with subtle horizontal and vertical lines indicating floor divisions and window frames. There is a central open area or courtyard between the buildings, and a small structure at the center, possibly representing an entrance or communal facility. The overall design is utilitarian and functional, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century urban apartment blocks.
description: A group of modern, high-rise apartment buildings with flat roofs and uniform window patterns, arranged around a central courtyard on a rectangular base. The complex features varying building heights and a clean, functional exterior.
description_view_0: Front view shows the long sides of the buildings with prominent window rows and the central courtyard visible between the structures.
description_view_1: This view highlights the taller buildings at the back and the lower, wider buildings in the foreground, emphasizing the depth and layout of the complex.
description_view_2: Side view reveals the narrow profiles of the buildings and the regular spacing between them, with window patterns visible on the sides.
description_view_3: Rear view displays the backs of the tallest buildings and the central open area, with the small central structure more visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in regular, evenly spaced vertical and horizontal rows, creating a symmetrical and grid-like pattern across all facades.
